scenario: Stellar Telco starts a fresh Camunda 8.8 service that will handle 5400 requests per day for a regional telco operator. A new Spring Boot project must be set up from scratch following the getting-started steps so that process variables can be bound to method parameter names without annotation values, before any application.yaml mode is configured for the orchestration cluster.
question: Which two Get started steps does the Camunda Spring Boot Starter page recommend before configuring the Orchestration Cluster connection?

options.text: Step 1 adds the io.camunda:camunda-spring-boot-starter dependency at 8.8.x to the build; Step 2 enables the Java compiler -parameters flag so process variables can be bound to method parameter names without setting an annotation value

Step 1 generates a new Spring Boot 3 project from the Camunda Initializr; Step 2 enables the @EnableCamundaClient annotation on the main class so the starter auto-wires the Camunda Java Client beans into the Spring context

Step 1 adds the io.camunda:zeebe-client-java dependency at 8.8.x; Step 2 sets the spring.profiles.active property to camunda so the starter scans the classpath for @JobWorker beans during application start-up

Step 1 adds the io.camunda:camunda-spring-zeebe-sdk dependency at 8.8.x; Step 2 configures the maven-compiler-plugin source level to 21 so process variables can be bound to method parameter names without further compiler flags

a.text: Correct. The Get started section starts with Step 1: add the io.camunda:camunda-spring-boot-starter dependency at 8.8.x. Step 2 enables the Java compiler -parameters flag so process variables can be bound to method parameter names without annotation values.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no Camunda Initializr or @EnableCamundaClient step on the page; the documented dependency is io.camunda:camunda-spring-boot-starter and the optional flag is the -parameters compiler argument.

c.text: Incorrect. The page does not point at the io.camunda:zeebe-client-java artifact for the starter path, and there is no documented spring.profiles.active=camunda toggle for worker scanning.

d.text: Incorrect. The Spring Zeebe SDK is being replaced and is not the documented dependency; the documented artifact is io.camunda:camunda-spring-boot-starter.

explanation: The Get started section lists Step 1: add the io.camunda:camunda-spring-boot-starter Maven dependency at version 8.8.x. Step 2 is optional and enables the Java compiler -parameters flag (in Maven, via the compilerArgs of maven-compiler-plugin), so process variables can be bound to method parameter names without specifying annotation values. Steps 3a and 3b then configure the cluster connection.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter getting started
